title: Peruvian Ají Verde

overview.description: A bright, creamy Peruvian green chilli sauce made with ají amarillo, coriander, lime and mayonnaise.

ingredients: 1 large handful fresh coriander, including tender stems
method: Add the ají amarillo paste, jalapeño, coriander, garlic and lime juice to a blender or food processor.
Add the mayonnaise, Greek yogurt and olive oil.
Taste and adjust the lime juice, salt and chilli to your preference.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavours to develop.

learning.did-you-know: Ají verde is one of the most recognisable sauces served with Peruvian-style roast chicken, although recipes vary between households and restaurants.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance chilli heat, acidity, herbs, creaminess and salt to make a versatile sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A versatile Peruvian sauce that introduces ají amarillo and adds a bright, spicy contrast to roast chicken and other rich foods.
chapman-tips: Ají amarillo paste is the most important ingredient for the characteristic flavour.
Use the coriander stems as well as the leaves; they contain plenty of flavour.
Start with less jalapeño if you are unsure about the heat and add more if needed.
The sauce will taste sharper immediately after blending but will mellow slightly after resting.
A little extra lime can brighten the sauce if it tastes too rich.
The sauce should be creamy but still have enough acidity and chilli to cut through rich roast chicken.
notes.batch-cooking: Make a larger batch if desired, but keep refrigerated and use within a few days. Freezing is not recommended because the mayonnaise and dairy can separate.
notes.leftovers: Use as a dipping sauce for chicken, roasted potatoes, chips or vegetables, or spread it into sandwiches and wraps.
